text:
Market today: Macro mood is cautious - gold selling off hard on rate fears signals the market is pricing in higher-for-longer rates. That's a headwind for high-multiple tech and chip names, exactly your book. No direct tech catalyst in today's headlines, so price action is the story.

Your holdings: All scanning HOLD/NEW - nothing is flashing red. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged chip ETF; on margin that's effectively massive amplification - any chip sector wobble hits hard and fast. SPAL is brand-new and very volatile; small moves feel big with margin underneath it. Everything else has comfortable cushion above stops, but stay honest with those levels.

Watch: 1) Rate-fear momentum - if bond yields keep climbing, high-multiple tech (CRWD, SMH, SOXL) will feel it first. 2) Oil wobble from Iran talks breakdown could ripple into energy costs for data centers, a softer chip demand signal.

Bottom line: Your book is fine today, but higher-for-longer rates are the single biggest risk to your concentrated tech/chip margin positions - protect the leveraged names.
plays: SOXL: Respect the $200 stop hard - you're on margin with a 3x levered chip ETF; if rates spike further, this unwinds fast. No reason to add here.
SPAL: Brand-new volatile leveraged ETF, small gain so far (+1.91%). Keep stop at $21.38 firm; no reason to size up until it proves itself with a few more days of price action.
No new buys today - scanner setups exist but today's rate-fear backdrop gives no real catalyst to enter fresh leveraged tech risk on margin.
